reason: The statement applies because the text describes a cycle of disobedience by the Israelites, leading to their oppression by the Midianites, a cry for help to the LORD, the sending of a prophet and an angel to deliver a message of rebuke and instruction, and finally the raising up of Gideon as a deliverer. This cycle reflects a pattern of the Hebrews not listening to the LORD or changing their ways until faced with dire circumstances.
